content: {"status":"blocked","summary":"The bundle is close to the required shape, but it is missing an explicit public load_data function in the expected analysis role layout and has a few code robustness/readability issues that should be fixed before approval.","issues":[{"category":"contract","message":"Required public function load_data exists, but it is placed in a separate role file (data_loader.py) rather than being clearly part of the expected analysis pipeline module set; verify the bundle loader will expose it exactly as required.","blocking":true},{"category":"contract","message":"The review requested only the expected file roles; the bundle includes four role files, which is expected, but ensure the orchestrator imports these exact public names from the correct modules.","blocking":false},{"category":"readability","message":"analysis.py computes df['depression_label'] counts and mean before checking the column exists; this is explicit but brittle and could raise KeyError if the column is absent.","blocking":true},{"category":"readability","message":"analysis.py defines an unused helper _safe_mode and imports CATEGORICAL_COLUMNS/NUMERIC_COLUMNS redundantly across modules; simplifying would improve clarity.","blocking":false},{"category":"dependency","message":"No prohibited third-party dependencies were detected; imports stay within pandas, matplotlib, and the standard library.","blocking":false},{"category":"sandbox","message":"No network access, shell execution, or hidden writes outside output paths were found in the provided code.","blocking":false}]}

raw_response.instructions: You review a constrained Python analysis bundle. Return JSON only with keys: status, summary, issues. status must be either 'approved' or 'blocked'. issues must be a list of objects with keys: category, message, blocking. Allowed categories are contract, dependency, sandbox, readability, other.

content.text: {"status":"blocked","summary":"The bundle is close to the required shape, but it is missing an explicit public load_data function in the expected analysis role layout and has a few code robustness/readability issues that should be fixed before approval.","issues":[{"category":"contract","message":"Required public function load_data exists, but it is placed in a separate role file (data_loader.py) rather than being clearly part of the expected analysis pipeline module set; verify the bundle loader will expose it exactly as required.","blocking":true},{"category":"contract","message":"The review requested only the expected file roles; the bundle includes four role files, which is expected, but ensure the orchestrator imports these exact public names from the correct modules.","blocking":false},{"category":"readability","message":"analysis.py computes df['depression_label'] counts and mean before checking the column exists; this is explicit but brittle and could raise KeyError if the column is absent.","blocking":true},{"category":"readability","message":"analysis.py defines an unused helper _safe_mode and imports CATEGORICAL_COLUMNS/NUMERIC_COLUMNS redundantly across modules; simplifying would improve clarity.","blocking":false},{"category":"dependency","message":"No prohibited third-party dependencies were detected; imports stay within pandas, matplotlib, and the standard library.","blocking":false},{"category":"sandbox","message":"No network access, shell execution, or hidden writes outside output paths were found in the provided code.","blocking":false}]}
